verdict_reasoning: LYN is an inner-leaflet-anchored Src-family kinase. Myristoylation at G2 and palmitoylation at C3 target LYN exclusively to the cytoplasmic (inner) face of the plasma membrane, where it associates with lipid rafts and cell-surface receptor signaling complexes (BCR, CD40, CD19). The protein body remains cytoplasmic-face-oriented throughout its lifecycle. While LYN has robust, documented plasma membrane localization driven by its own lipidation, it does not reach the outer leaflet where binders from the extracellular space could engage it.
